name: Linked List Cycle
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if it has a cycle. A cycle exists if some node can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer. Use Floyd's tortoise and hare algorithm.

examples.input: head = [3,2,0,-4], pos = 1 (tail connects to node index 1)

constraints: The number of nodes is in [0, 10^4]. -10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5. pos is -1 or a valid index.
python_template:

performance.stress_intent: exercise long acyclic and cyclic lists that punish nested rescans or per-node visited checks implemented with repeated traversal
